reference_html:
TECHNOLOGY · 5 MIN READ

The Rise of AI-Assisted Development

AJ

Amal Joe

April 8, 2026

"AI is not replacing developers — it's giving them superpowers."

Modern development has shifted dramatically with the introduction of large language models capable of understanding and generating code. Teams that once spent days on boilerplate now ship features in hours.

The key insight is that AI excels at pattern completion, while humans excel at problem definition. Together, the combination is unbeatable.

Streamline
Features Pricing Docs
NOW IN BETA

Ship faster,
break nothing.

The all-in-one platform for teams that care about quality. CI/CD, monitoring, and deployment in one place.

⚡

Fast Deploys

Push to production in under 30 seconds with zero downtime.

🔒

Secure by Default

SOC2 compliant. Secrets management and audit logs built in.

📊

Live Monitoring

Real-time metrics, error tracking, and alerting out of the box.

reference_html:
DevDigest
Articles Newsletter Jobs
RUST

Why Rust is Winning the Systems Programming War

By Alex Kim · 8 min read · Apr 8, 2026

Memory safety without garbage collection was once considered impossible. Rust has proved the skeptics wrong, and major tech companies are taking notice.

The Linux kernel, Android, and the Windows kernel have all started incorporating Rust. This isn't a trend — it's a fundamental shift in how critical infrastructure is built.

The Ownership Model

Rust's ownership system enforces memory safety at compile time. Every value has exactly one owner, and the compiler ensures that references never outlive their data.

Trending

1. Go vs Rust in 2026

2. The React Server Components Guide

3. Postgres vs SQLite at scale

4. Building your own LLM

Newsletter

Weekly digest of the best dev articles.

reference_html:
Docs
Guide API Reference Examples
🔍 Search docs...

Getting Started

Introduction Installation Quick Start

Core Concepts

Environments Actions & Obs Rewards

API

POST /reset POST /step GET /state
Docs›Getting Started›Introduction

Introduction

OpenEnv is a standard HTTP interface for building reinforcement learning environments. It provides a simple REST API that any agent can interact with, regardless of programming language.

INSTALLATION

pip install openenv-core

How it works

Each environment exposes three endpoints: /reset to start an episode, /step to submit an action, and /state to inspect current state.
